1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: AN INQUIRER ASKED for the affliction that is to come

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: A requester seeks the punishment that will take place –

1.author: Arberry
1.text: A questioner asked of a chastisement about to fall

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: There hath asked an asker for the torment about to befall.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: A questioner asked concerning a torment about to befall

1.author: Itani
1.text: A questioner questioned the imminent torment.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: A beseecher besought the visitation of chastisement,

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: A questioner asked concerning a torment about to befall

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: A questioner questioned concerning the doom about to fall

1.author: Qarai
1.text: An asker asked for a punishment sure to befall

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: A caller supplicated about a punishment to fall on

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: A supplicant asked for a punishment bound to happen

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: Someone has (needlessly) demanded to experience the torment (of God),

1.author: Shakir
1.text: One demanding, demanded the chastisement which must befall

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: A doubter once demanded that punishment be immediately meted out,

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: A questioner asked about a Penalty to befall-

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: Upon the infidels -- which none would be able to repel --

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: - Upon the disbelievers – the punishment that none can avert.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: for the unbelievers, which none may avert,

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: The infidels, of which there is no averter.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Upon the disbelievers, which none can avert,

2.author: Itani
2.text: For the disbelievers; none can repel it.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: (a chastisement meant) for the unbelievers, one which none can avert;

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: Upon the disbelievers, which none can avert,

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: Upon the disbelievers, which none can repel,

2.author: Qarai
2.text: —which none can avert from the faithless —

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: the unbelievers which none can prevent.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: To the disbelievers; of it there is no preventer.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: which will inevitably seize the disbelievers.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: The unbelievers-- there is none to avert it--

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: to those who deny the truth. No power can hinder God

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: The Unbelievers, the which there is none to ward off,-

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: From God, the Lord of the steps (of progression),

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: From Allah, the Lord of all pinnacles.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: from God, the Lord of the Stairways.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: From Allah, Owner of the ascending steps.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: From Allah, the Lord of the ways of ascent.

3.author: Itani
3.text: From God, Lord of the Ways of Ascent.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: a chastisement from Allah, the Lord of the ascending steps,

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: From Allah, the Lord of the ways of ascent.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: From Allah, Lord of the Ascending Stairways

3.author: Qarai
3.text: from Allah, Lord of the lofty stations.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: (A punishment) from Allah, the Owner of the Elevated Passages.

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: [It is] from Allah, owner of the ways of ascent.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: No one can defend him against God, the Lord of the exalted positions.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: From Allah, the Lord of the ways of Ascent.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: from punishing them. He is the Lord of the Ascending Stairways,

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: (A Penalty) from Allah, Lord of the Ways of Ascent.

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: To whom the angels and the soul take a day to ascend, whose length is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: The angels and Jibreel, ascend towards Him – the punishment will befall on a day which spans fifty thousand years.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: To Him the angels and the Spirit mount up, in a day whereof the measure is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: Whereby the angels ascend Unto Him and also the spirit, On a Day whereof the measure is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: The angels and the Ruh [Jibrael (Gabriel)] ascend to Him in a Day the measure whereof is fifty thousand years,

4.author: Itani
4.text: Unto Him the angels and the Spirit ascend on a Day the duration of which is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: by which the angels and the Spirit ascend to Him in one Day the duration of which is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: The angels and the Ruh ascend to Him in a Day the measure whereof is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: (Whereby) the angels and the Spirit ascend unto Him in a Day whereof the span is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: The angels and the Spirit ascend to Him in a day whose span is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: To Him the angels and the Spirit (Gabriel) ascend in a day, the measure of which is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: The angels and the Spirit will ascend to Him during a Day the extent of which is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: On that Day (of Judgment), long as fifty thousand years, the angels and the Spirit will ascend to Him.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: To Him ascend the angels and the Spirit in a day the measure of which is fifty thousand years.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: by which the angels and the Spirit will ascend to Him in one Day which will last for fifty thousand years.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: The angels and the spirit ascend unto him in a Day the measure whereof is (as) fifty thousand years:

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: TaAAruju almalaikatu waalrroohu ilayhi fee yawmin kana miqdaruhu khamseena alfa sanatin

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: Therefore patiently endure, in the best manner (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him).

5.author: Arberry
5.text: So be thou patient with a sweet patience;

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Wherefore be thou patient with a becoming patience.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: So be patient (O Muhammad SAW), with a good patience.

5.author: Itani
5.text: So be patient, with sweet patience.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: So, (O Prophet), persevere with gracious perseverance.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: So be patient, with a good patience.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: But be patient (O Muhammad) with a patience fair to see.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: So be patient, with a patience that is graceful.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: Therefore be patient, with a beautiful patience;

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: So be patient with gracious patience.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Therefore endure with a goodly patience.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: Therefore, [O believers] behave with seemly patience.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: Therefore do thou hold Patience,- a Patience of beautiful (contentment).

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: They surely take it to be far away,

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: They deem it to be remote.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: behold, they see it as if far off;

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: Verily they behold it afar off.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: Verily! They see it (the torment) afar off,

6.author: Maududi
6.text: Verily they think that the chastisement is far off,

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: Verily, they see it (the torment) afar off.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Lo! they behold it afar off

6.author: Qarai
6.text: Indeed they see it to be far off,

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: they see it as being far off;

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: They think that it (the Day of Judgment) is far away.

6.author: Shakir
6.text: Surely they think it to be far off,

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: They see it [the Day of Judgement] to be far off,

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: They see the (Day) indeed as a far-off (event):

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: But We see it very near.

7.author: Arberry
7.text: but We see it is nigh.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: while We think that it is near at hand.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: and We see it to be near.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: but We see it to be very near.

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: but We see it near at hand.

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: The day the sky becomes like molten brass,

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: The day when the sky will be like molten silver.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: Upon the day when heaven shall be as molten copper

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: It shall befall on a Day whereon the heaven shall become like Unto dregs of oil.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: The Day that the sky will be like the boiling filth of oil, (or molten copper or silver or lead, etc.).

8.author: Itani
8.text: On the Day when the sky will be like molten brass.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: It shall befall on a Day whereon the sky will become like molten brass,

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: The Day that the sky will be like the Al-Muhl.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: The day when the sky will become as molten copper,

8.author: Qarai
8.text: The day when the sky will be like molten copper,

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: On that Day the heaven shall become like molten copper,

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: On the Day the sky will be like murky oil,

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: On the day when the heavens become like molten metal.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: On the day when the heaven shall be as molten copper

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: On that Day the heavens shall become like molten brass,

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: The Day that the sky will be like molten brass,

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: The mountains like the tufts of (carded) wool,

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: And the hills will be light as wool.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: and the mountains shall be as plucked wool-tufts,

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: And then the mountains shall become like Unto wool dyed.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: And the mountains will be like flakes of wool,

9.author: Itani
9.text: And the mountains will be like tufted wool.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: and the mountains will become like dyed tufts of wool,

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: And the mountains will be like `Ihn.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: And the hills become as flakes of wool,

9.author: Qarai
9.text: and the mountains like [tufts of] dyed wool,

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: and the mountains shall be like puffs of wool.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: And the mountains will be like wool,

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: and the mountains become like wool,

9.author: Shakir
9.text: And the mountains shall be as tufts of wool

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: and the mountains will become like tufts of wool,

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: And the mountains will be like wool,

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: And no friend inquires after friend

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: And no friend will ask concerning his friend.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: no loyal friend shall question loyal friend,

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: And not a friend shall ask a friend,

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: And no friend will ask of a friend,

10.author: Itani
10.text: No friend will care about his friend.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: and no bosom friend will enquire about any of his bosom friends

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: And no friend will ask a friend,

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: And no familiar friend will ask a question of his friend

10.author: Qarai
10.text: and no friend will inquire about [the welfare of his] friend,

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: No loyal friend will ask another loyal friend

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: And no friend will ask [anything of] a friend,

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: even intimate friends will not inquire about their friends,

10.author: Shakir
10.text: And friend shall not ask of friend

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: and no friend will ask about his friend,

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: And no friend will ask after a friend,

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: Though within sight of one another. The sinner would like to ransom himself from the torment of that Day by offering his sons,

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: They will be seeing them; the guilty will wish if only he could redeem himself from the punishment of that day, by offering his sons.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: as they are given sight of them. The sinner will wish that he might ransom himself from the chastisement of that day even by his sons,

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Though they shall be made to see one another. Fain would the guilty ransom himself from the torment of that Day by his children.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Though they shall be made to see one another [(i.e. on the Day of Resurrection), there will be none but see his father, children and relatives, but he will neither speak to them nor will ask them for any help)], - the Mujrim, (criminal, sinner, disbeliever, etc.) would desire to ransom himself from the punishment of that Day by his children.

11.author: Itani
11.text: They will be shown each other. The criminal wishes he would be redeemed from the punishment of that Day by his children.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: although they shall be within sight of one another. The guilty one would fain ransom himself from the torment of that Day by offering his children,

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Though they shall be made to see one another, the criminal would desire to ransom himself from the punishment of that Day by his children.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Though they will be given sight of them. The guilty man will long to be able to ransom himself from the punishment of that day at the price of his children

11.author: Qarai
11.text: [though] they will be placed within each other’s sight. The guilty one will wish he could ransom himself from the punishment of that day at the price of his children,

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: though they are in sight of each other. To ransom himself from the punishment of that Day, the sinner will wish that he might even ransom himself by his sons,

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: They will be shown each other. The criminal will wish that he could be ransomed from the punishment of that Day by his children

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: though they may see each other. A sinner will wish that he could save himself from the torment of that day by sacrificing his children,

11.author: Shakir
11.text: (Though) they shall be made to see each other. The guilty one would fain redeem himself from the chastisement of that day by (sacrificing) his children,

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: though they shall be within sight of each other. The guilty one will gladly ransom himself from the torment of that Day by sacrificing his own children,

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: Though they will be put in sight of each other,- the sinner's desire will be: Would that he could redeem himself from the Penalty of that Day by (sacrificing) his children,

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Yubassaroonahum yawaddu almujrimu law yaftadee min AAathabi yawmiithin bibaneehi

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: And his wife and his brother.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: And his spouse and his brother.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: And his wife and his brother,

12.author: Itani
12.text: And his spouse, and his brother.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: and his spouse and his brother,

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: And his wife and his brother,

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: And his spouse and his brother

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: And his wife and his brother

12.author: Shakir
12.text: And his wife and his brother

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: And his family who had stood by him,

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And the family in which he was.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And his kin that sheltered him.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And his kindred who sheltered him,

13.author: Itani
13.text: And his family that sheltered him.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: and his kinsfolk who had stood by him,

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: And his Fasilah who sheltered him,

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And his kin that harboured him

13.author: Qarai
13.text: his kin, which had sheltered him

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: the kinsmen who gave him shelter,

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And his nearest kindred who shelter him

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: his kinsmen who gave him refuge (from hardship)

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And the nearest of his kinsfolk who gave him shelter,

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: and his kinsfolk who gave him shelter,

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: And all those who are on the earth, to save himself.

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: And all those who are in the earth – then only if the redemption saves him!

14.author: Arberry
14.text: and whosoever is in the earth, all together, so that then it might deliver him.

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: And all who are on the earth; so that this might deliver him.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: And all that are in the earth, so that it might save him.

14.author: Itani
14.text: And everyone on earth, in order to save him.

14.author: Maududi
14.text: and all persons of the earth, if only he could thus save himself.

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: And all that are in the earth, so that it might save him.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: And all that are in the earth, if then it might deliver him.

14.author: Qarai
14.text: and all those who are upon the earth, if that might deliver him.

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: and whosoever is in the earth, altogether, so that it might save him.

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: And whoever is on earth entirely [so] then it could save him.

14.author: Shakir
14.text: And all those that are in the earth, (wishing) then (that) this might deliver him.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: and all the people of the earth, if that could deliver him.

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: And all, all that is on earth,- so it could deliver him:

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: Waman fee alardi jameeAAan thumma yunjeehi

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: But never. It is pure white flame

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Never! That is indeed a blazing fire.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: Nay, verily it is a furnace

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: By no means! Verily it is a Flame.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: By no means! Verily, it will be the Fire of Hell!

15.author: Itani
15.text: By no means! It is a Raging Fire.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: By no means! It will be the fierce flame

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: By no means! Verily, it will be the fire of Hell,

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: But nay! for lo! it is the fire of hell

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Never! Indeed, it is a blazing fire,

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: No, in truth, it is a Furnace!

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: No! Indeed, it is the Flame [of Hell],

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: By no means! For the raging flames of the fire

15.author: Shakir
15.text: By no means! Surely it is a flaming fire

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: But no! There is a raging blaze

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: By no means! for it would be the Fire of Hell!-

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: A fire that melts the hide.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: Taking away (burning completely) the head skin!

16.author: Maududi
16.text: that will strip off the scalp.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: It snatches away by the scalps,

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: Plucking out (his being) right to the skull!-

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: It will summon whoever turns his back and flees,

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: It calls out to him who reverted and turned away.

17.author: Arberry
17.text: calling him who drew back and turned away,

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: It shall calll him who turneth back and backslideth.

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Calling: "[O Kafir (O disbeliever in Allah, His angels, His Book, His Messengers, Day of Resurrection and in Al-Qadar (Divine Preordainments), O Mushrik (O polytheist, disbeliever in the Oneness of Allah)] (all) such as turn their backs and turn away their faces (from Faith) [picking and swallowing them up from that great gathering of mankind (on the Day of Resurrection) just as a bird picks up a food-grain from the earth with its beak and swallows it up] [Tafsir Al-Qurtubi, Vol. 18, Page 289]

17.author: Itani
17.text: It invites him who once turned his back and fled.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: It shall insistently summon him who turned his back and retreated,

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Calling (all) such as turn their backs and turn away their faces.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: It calleth him who turned and fled (from truth),

17.author: Qarai
17.text: It invites him, who has turned back [from the truth] and forsaken [it],

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: and it shall call him who withdrew and turned his back

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: It invites he who turned his back [on truth] and went away [from obedience]

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: and drag into it anyone who has turned away (from obeying God),

17.author: Shakir
17.text: It shall claim him who turned and fled (from truth),

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: and it will claim all those who turned their backs [on the true faith] and turned away [from the truth],

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Inviting (all) such as turn their backs and turn away their faces (from the Right).

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: And accumulated wealth and hoarded it.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: And collect (wealth) and hide it (from spending it in the Cause of Allah).

18.author: Maududi
18.text: and amassed wealth and covetously hoarded it.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: And collect (wealth) and hide it (from spending it in the cause of Allah).

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: and who accumulated wealth without spending it for a good purpose.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: And amasses (wealth) then shuts it up.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: and amassed wealth and hoarded it.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: And collect (wealth) and hide it (from use)!

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: Surely man is greedy by nature.

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: Indeed man is created very impatient, greedy.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: Verily, man (disbeliever) was created very impatient;

19.author: Maududi
19.text: Verily man is impatient by nature:

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: Verily, man was created very impatient;

19.author: Qarai
19.text: Indeed man has been created covetous:

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: Indeed, the human was created grudging and impatient.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: Surely man is created of a hasty temperament

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: Truly man was created very impatient;-

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: If evil befalls him he is perturbed;

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: Very nervous when touched by misfortune.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: When evil toucheth him, he is bewailing.

20.author: Itani
20.text: Touched by adversity, he is fretful.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: anxious when an ill befalls him

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: When evil comes upon him he is impatient;

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: When they are afflicted, they complain,

20.author: Shakir
20.text: Being greatly grieved when evil afflicts him

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: when misfortune touches him he starts lamenting,

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: If good comes to him he holds back his hand,

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: And refraining, when good reaches him.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: And when good toucheth him he is begrudging.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: And niggardly when good touches him;

21.author: Itani
21.text: Touched by good, he is ungenerous.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: and tight-fisted when good fortune visits him,

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: And suppressive when good touches him.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: And, when good befalleth him, grudging;

21.author: Qarai
21.text: and grudging [charity] when good comes his way

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: but when good comes upon him, he is grudging

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: And when good touches him, withholding [of it],

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: but when they are fortunate, they become niggardly

21.author: Shakir
21.text: And niggardly when good befalls him

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: and whenever good fortune comes to him, he grows niggardly.

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: And niggardly when good reaches him;-

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: Except those who closely follow (the Book of God),

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: Except those who are devoted to Salah.

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: Not so those devoted to Prayer;-

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: Those who are regular in their prayers.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: Who are at their prayer constant.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: Those who remain constant in their Salat (prayers);

23.author: Itani
23.text: Those who are constant at their prayers.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: and are constant in their Prayer;

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: Those who with their Salah are Da'imun;

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: Who are constant at their worship

23.author: Qarai
23.text: those who persevere in their prayers

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: Those who are constant in their prayer

23.author: Shakir
23.text: Those who are constant at their prayer

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: Those who remain steadfast to their prayer;

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: In whose wealth a due share is included

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: And those in whose wealth exists a recognised right,

24.author: Arberry
24.text: those in whose wealth is a right known

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: And those in whose riches is a known right.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: And those in whose wealth there is a known right,

24.author: Itani
24.text: And those in whose wealth is a rightful share.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: and those in whose wealth there is a known right

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: And those in whose wealth there is a recognized right.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: And in whose wealth there is a right acknowledged

24.author: Qarai
24.text: and there is a known share in whose wealth

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: who, from their wealth is a known right

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: And those within whose wealth is a known right

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: They are those who assign a certain share of their property

24.author: Shakir
24.text: And those in whose wealth there is a fixed portion.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: those who give a due share of their wealth

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: And those in whose wealth is a recognised right.

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: For the needy and those dispossessed,

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: For those who beg, and for the needy who cannot even ask.

25.author: Arberry
25.text: for the beggar and the outcast,

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: For the beggar and the destitute.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: For the beggar who asks, and for the unlucky who has lost his property and wealth, (and his means of living has been straitened);

25.author: Itani
25.text: For the beggar and the deprived.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: for those that ask and those that are dispossessed,

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: For the one who asks, and for the deprived.

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: For the beggar and the destitute;

25.author: Qarai
25.text: for the beggar and the deprived,

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: for the impoverished nonrequester and the requester,

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: For the petitioner and the deprived -

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: for the needy and the deprived,

25.author: Shakir
25.text: For him who begs and for him who is denied (good)

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: to those who ask [for help] and to the destitute;

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: For the (needy) who asks and him who is prevented (for some reason from asking);

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: And those who believe in the Day of Judgement,

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: And those who believe the Day of Judgement to be true.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: who confirm the Day of Doom

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: And those who testify to the Day of Requital.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: And those who believe in the Day of Recompense,

26.author: Itani
26.text: And those who affirm the Day of Judgment.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: those who firmly believe in the Day of Recompense,

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: And those who believe in the Day of Recompense.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: And those who believe in the Day of Judgment,

26.author: Qarai
26.text: and who affirm the Day of Retribution,

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: who confirm the Day of Reckoning

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: And those who believe in the Day of Recompense

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: who acknowledge the Day of Judgment,

26.author: Shakir
26.text: And those who accept the truth of the judgment day

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: and those who believe in the Day of Judgement

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: And those who hold to the truth of the Day of Judgment;

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: And those who fear the punishment of their Lord, --

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And those who fear the punishment of their Lord.

27.author: Arberry
27.text: and go in fear of the chastisement of their Lord

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: And those who are fearful of their Lord's torment -

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And those who fear the torment of their Lord,

27.author: Itani
27.text: And those who fear the punishment of their Lord.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: and fear the chastisement of their Lord –

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And those who fear the torment of their Lord.

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: And those who are fearful of their Lord's doom -

27.author: Qarai
27.text: and those who are apprehensive of the punishment of their Lord

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: and go in fear of the punishment of their Lord,

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And those who are fearful of the punishment of their Lord -

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: who are afraid of the torment of their Lord,

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And those who are fearful of the chastisement of their Lord--

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: and are fearful of the punishment of their Lord;

27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: And those who fear the displeasure of their Lord,-

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Waallatheena hum min AAathabi rabbihim mushfiqoona

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: Surely no one can be secure from the punishment of his Lord, --

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: Indeed the punishment of their Lord is not a thing to be unafraid of!

28.author: Arberry
28.text: (from their Lord's chastisement none feels secure)

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: Verily the torment of their Lord is not a thing to feel secure from.

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: Verily! The torment of their Lord is that before which none can feel secure,

28.author: Itani
28.text: Their Lord’s punishment is not to be taken for granted.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: surely the chastisement of their Lord is a thing none can feel secure from –

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: Verily, the torment of their Lord is that before which none can feel secure.

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: Lo! the doom of their Lord is that before which none can feel secure -

28.author: Qarai
28.text: (there is indeed no security from the punishment of their Lord)

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: for none is secure from the punishment of their Lord,

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: Indeed, the punishment of their Lord is not that from which one is safe -

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: the punishment of their Lord is not something for them to feel secure of,

28.author: Shakir
28.text: Surely the chastisement of their Lord is (a thing) not to be felt secure of--

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: for none may ever feel secure from the punishment of their Lord;

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: For their Lord's displeasure is the opposite of Peace and Tranquillity;-

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: And those who guard their sex except from their wives and women slaves of old

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: And those who protect their private organs (from adultery).

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: And those who of their private parts are guards.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: And those who guard their chastity (i.e. private parts from illegal sexual acts).

29.author: Itani
29.text: And those who guard their chastity.

29.author: Maududi
29.text: and those who guard their private parts,

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: And those who guard their private part (chastity).

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: And those who preserve their chastity

29.author: Qarai
29.text: and those who guard their private parts

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: And those who guard their private parts

29.author: Shakir
29.text: And those who guard their private parts,

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: And those who guard their chastity,

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: Except with their wives and the bondwomen in their possession, for there is no reproach on them.

30.author: Arberry
30.text: save from their wives and what their right hands own, then not being blameworthy

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: Save in regard to their spouses or those whom their right hands own; so verily they are not blameworthy -

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: Except with their wives and the (women slaves and captives) whom their right hands possess, for (then) they are not to be blamed,

30.author: Itani
30.text: Except from their spouses or those living under their control, for then they are free of blame.

30.author: Maududi
30.text: except in regard to their spouses and those whom their right hands possess, for in regard to them they are not reproachable,

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: Except from their wives or their right hand possessions -- for (then) they are not blameworthy.

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: Save with their wives and those whom their right hands possess, for thus they are not blameworthy;

30.author: Qarai
30.text: (apart from their spouses and their slave women, for then they are not blameworthy;

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: except from their wives and what their right hands own, for these they are not blameworthy.

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: Except from their wives or those their right hands possess, for indeed, they are not to be blamed -

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: except from their wives and slave girls, in which case they are not to be blamed,

30.author: Shakir
30.text: Except in the case of their wives or those whom their right hands possess-- for these surely are not to be blamed,

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: except from their spouses and those whom they rightfully possess [through wedlock], for which they incur no blame --

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: Except with their wives and the (captives) whom their right hands possess,- for (then) they are not to be blamed,

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Illa AAala azwajihim aw ma malakat aymanuhum fainnahum ghayru maloomeena

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: But those who seek more than this will be transgressors;

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: So those who desire more than this – it is they who are the transgressors.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: (but whoso seeks after more than that, they are the transgressors),

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And whosoever seeketh beyond that, then it is those who are the trespassers

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: But whosoever seeks beyond that, then it is those who are trespassers.

31.author: Itani
31.text: But whoever seeks to go beyond that—these are the transgressors.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: but any who seeks to go beyond that, it is indeed they who are the transgressors,

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: But whosoever seeks beyond that, then it is those who are trespassers.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: But whoso seeketh more than that, those are they who are transgressors;

31.author: Qarai
31.text: but whoever seeks beyond that—it is they who are the transgressors)

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: But whosoever seeks beyond that, they are the transgressors.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: But whoever seeks beyond that, then they are the transgressors -

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: but whoever goes beyond this is a transgressor;

31.author: Shakir
31.text: But he who seeks to go beyond this, these it is that go beyond the limits--

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: but those who go beyond that limit are transgressors;

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: But those who trespass beyond this are transgressors;-

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Famani ibtagha waraa thalika faolaika humu alAAadoona

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: And those who fulfil their trusts and covenants,

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: And those who protect the property entrusted to them, and their agreements.

32.author: Arberry
32.text: and who preserve their trusts and their covenant,

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And those who of their trusts and their covenant are keepers.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: And those who keep their trusts and covenants;

32.author: Itani
32.text: And those who honor their trusts and their pledges.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: and those who fulfil their trusts and their covenants,

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: And those who keep their trusts and covenants.

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And those who keep their pledges and their covenant,

32.author: Qarai
32.text: and those who keep their trusts and covenants,

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: (Those) who keep their trusts and their covenant,

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And those who are to their trusts and promises attentive

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: who honor their trust and promises,

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And those who are faithful to their trusts and their covenant

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: and those who are faithful to their trusts and to their pledges;

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: And those who respect their trusts and covenants;

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: And those who are firm upon their testimonies.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: And those who stand firm in their testimonies.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: And those who stand firm in their testimonies;

33.author: Itani
33.text: And those who stand by their testimonies.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: and those who are upright in their testimonies;

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: And those who stand firm in their testimonies.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: And those who stand by their testimony

33.author: Qarai
33.text: and those who are observant of their testimonies,

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: And those who are in their testimonies upright

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: who testify to what they have witnessed,

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And those who are upright in their testimonies,

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: and those who stand by their testimony

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: And those who stand firm in their testimonies;

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: And those who are mindful of their moral obligations.

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: And those who protect their prayers.

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: And those who of their prayer are observant.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: And those who guard their Salat (prayers) well.

34.author: Itani
34.text: And those who are dedicated to their prayers.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: and who take due care of their Prayer:

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: And those who are with their Salah, Yuhafizun.

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: And those who are attentive at their worship.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: and those who are watchful of their prayers.

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: and who are constant in their prayers.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: And those who [carefully] maintain their prayer:

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: and (finally) those who do not miss their ritual - prayers at the prescribed times;

34.author: Shakir
34.text: And those who keep a guard on their prayer,

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: and are steadfast in their prayers.

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: And those who guard (the sacredness) of their worship;-

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: They will live in gardens with honour.

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: It is these who will be honoured in Gardens.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: Those shall be in Gardens, high-honoured.

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: Those shall dwell in Gardens, honoured.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: Such shall dwell in the Gardens (i.e. Paradise) honoured.

35.author: Itani
35.text: These will be honored in Gardens.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: all these shall live honourably in the Gardens.

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: Such shall dwell in the Gardens, honored

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: These will dwell in Gardens, honoured.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: They will be in gardens, held in honour.

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: Those are highly honored in the Gardens.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: They will be in gardens, honored.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: such people will receive due honor in Paradise.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: Those shall be in gardens, honored.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: They will be honoured in the Gardens of Bliss.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: Such will be the honoured ones in the Gardens (of Bliss).

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: What is the matter with unbelievers that they stare at you with fixed gazes and hasten towards you

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: So what is the matter with these disbelievers, that they stare at you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him)?

36.author: Arberry
36.text: What ails the unbelievers, running with outstretched necks

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: What aileth those who disbelieve, toward thee hastening.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: So what is the matter with those who disbelieve that they hasten to listen from you (O Muhammad SAW), in order to belie you and to mock at you, and at Allah's Book (this Quran).

36.author: Itani
36.text: What is with those who disbelieve, stretching their necks towards you.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: But what is the matter with the unbelievers who are hurrying towards you

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: So, what is the matter with those disbelievers, before you Muhti`in

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: What aileth those who disbelieve, that they keep staring toward thee (O Muhammad), open-eyed,

36.author: Qarai
36.text: What is the matter with the faithless that they scramble toward you,

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: But what is the matter with the unbelievers, that they strain their glances continuously towards them,

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: So what is [the matter] with those who disbelieve, hastening [from] before you, [O Muhammad],

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: What is wrong with the disbelievers who roam around you (Muhammad),

36.author: Shakir
36.text: But what is the matter with those who disbelieve that they hasten on around you,

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: But what is the matter with those who deny the truth, that they come hastening towards you

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: Now what is the matter with the Unbelievers that they rush madly before thee-

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: From the right and the left, in groups?

37.author: Arberry
37.text: towards thee on the right hand and on the left hand in knots?

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: On the right and on the left, in companies?

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: (Sitting) in groups on the right and on the left (of you, O Muhammad SAW)?

37.author: Itani
37.text: From the right, and from the left, banding together?

37.author: Maududi
37.text: in crowds, both on the right and on the left?

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: On the right and on the left, `Izin.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: On the right and on the left, in groups?

37.author: Qarai
37.text: from left and right, in groups?

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: on the right and on the left in groups?

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: [To sit] on [your] right and [your] left in separate groups?

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: left and right, in numerous groups?

37.author: Shakir
37.text: On the right hand and on the left, in sundry parties?

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: from the right and from the left, in crowds?

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: From the right and from the left, in crowds?

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: Does every one of them wish to enter the garden of tranquility?

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: Does every man among them aspire to be admitted into the Garden of serenity?

38.author: Arberry
38.text: What, is every man of them eager to be admitted to a Garden of Bliss?

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: Coveteth every man of them, that he shall enter the Garden of Delight?

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: Does every man of them hope to enter the Paradise of delight?

38.author: Itani
38.text: Is every one of them aspiring to be admitted into a Garden of Bliss?

38.author: Maududi
38.text: Does everyone of them wish to enter the Garden of Bliss?

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: Does every man of them hope to enter the Paradise of Delight

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: Doth every man among them hope to enter the Garden of Delight?

38.author: Qarai
38.text: Does each man among them hope to enter the garden of bliss?

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: What, is every one of them eager to be admitted to the Garden of Bliss?

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: Does every person among them aspire to enter a garden of pleasure?

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: Does every one of them desire to enter the bountiful Paradise?

38.author: Shakir
38.text: Does every man of them desire that he should be made to enter the garden of bliss?

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: Does every one of them aspire to be admitted into a Garden of Delight?

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: Does every man of them long to enter the Garden of Bliss?

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: AyatmaAAu kullu imriin minhum an yudkhala jannata naAAeemin

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: Never so. We have created them from what they know.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: Never! We have indeed created them from a thing they know.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: Not so; for We have created them of what they know.

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: By no means! veriiy We! We have created them from that which they know.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: No, that is not like that! Verily, We have created them out of that which they know!

39.author: Itani
39.text: No indeed! We created them from what they know.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: By no means! They know that which We have created them from.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: But no! Verily, We have created them out of that which they know!

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: Nay, verily. Lo! We created them from what they know.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: Never! Indeed, We created them from what they know.

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: Not at all, for We have created them of what they know.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: No! Indeed, We have created them from that which they know.

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: By no means! For they know very well out of what We have created them (The human being's naturally growing from a living germ, without discipline and good deeds will not result in virtue).

39.author: Shakir
39.text: By no means! Surely We have created them of what they know.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: Certainly not! They know quite well out of what We created them.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: By no means! For We have created them out of the (base matter) they know!

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: So I swear by the Lord of the Easts and the Wests that We are certainly able

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: So I swear by the Lord of every East and every West, that We are indeed Able.

40.author: Arberry
40.text: No! I swear by the Lord of the Easts and Wests, surely We are able

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: I swear by the Lord of the easts and the wests, that, vierly We are Able.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: So I swear by the Lord of all [the three hundred and sixty (360)] points of sunrise and sunset in the east and the west that surely We are Able

40.author: Itani
40.text: I swear by the Lord of the Easts and the Wests, that We are Able.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: I swear by the Lord of the easts and the wests that We have the power

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: But no! I swear by the Lord of the easts and the wests that surely We are Able

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: But nay! I swear by the Lord of the rising-places and the setting-places of the planets that We verily are Able

40.author: Qarai
40.text: So I swear by the Lord of the easts and the wests that We are able

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: No, I swear by the Lord of the Easts and the Wests that We have the ability

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: So I swear by the Lord of [all] risings and settings that indeed We are able

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: I do not need to swear by the Lord of the eastern and western regions that We have certainly all the power

40.author: Shakir
40.text: But nay! I swear by the Lord of the Easts and the Wests that We are certainly able

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: But nay! I call to witness the Lord of the Easts and the Wests, that We have the power

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: Now I do call to witness the Lord of all points in the East and the West that We can certainly-

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Fala oqsimu birabbi almashariqi waalmagharibi inna laqadiroona

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: To bring better people than they in their place; and they will not be able to thwart Us.

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: To replace them by those better than them; and none can escape from Us.

41.author: Arberry
41.text: to substitute a better than they; We shall not be outstripped.

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: To replace them by others better than they; and We are not to be outrun.

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: To replace them by (others) better than them; and We are not to be outrun.

41.author: Itani
41.text: To replace them with better than they, and We are not to be outdone.

41.author: Maududi
41.text: to replace them by others who would be better than they; and We shall certainly not be overpowered.

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: To replace them by (others) better than them; and We are not to be outrun.

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: To replace them by (others) better than them. And we are not to be outrun.

41.author: Qarai
41.text: to replace them with [others] better than them and We are not to be outmaneuvered.

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: to exchange them with others better than they; nothing can outstrip Us!

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: To replace them with better than them; and We are not to be outdone.

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: to replace them by a better people and none can challenge Our power.

41.author: Shakir
41.text: To bring instead (others) better than them, and We shall not be overcome.

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: to replace them with others better than them: nothing can prevent Us from doing this,

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: Substitute for them better (men) than they; And We are not to be defeated (in Our Plan).

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: AAala an nubaddila khayran minhum wama nahnu bimasbooqeena

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: So leave them to their vain disputes and amusement till they meet their day (of reckoning) promised them,

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: Therefore leave them, involved in their indecencies and play, till they confront their day which they are promised.

42.author: Arberry
42.text: Then leave them alone to plunge and play until they encounter that day of theirs which they are promised,

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Wherefore let thou them alone plunging in vanity and sporting until they meet their Day which they are promised.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: So leave them to plunge in vain talk and play about, until they meet their Day which they are promised.

42.author: Itani
42.text: So leave them to blunder and play, until they meet their Day which they are promised.

42.author: Maududi
42.text: So leave them to engage in vain talk and to amuse themselves until they come face to face with the Day which they are promised,

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: So, leave them to plunge in vain talk and play about, until they meet their Day which they are promised.

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: So let them chat and play until they meet their Day which they are promised,

42.author: Qarai
42.text: So leave them to gossip and play till they encounter the day they are promised:

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: So leave them alone to plunge and play, until they encounter the Day which they are promised;

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: So leave them to converse vainly and amuse themselves until they meet their Day which they are promised -

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: (Muhammad), leave them alone to dispute and play until they face the Day with which they have been threatened:

42.author: Shakir
42.text: Therefore leave them alone to go on with the false discourses and to sport until they come face to face with that day of theirs with which they are threatened;

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: so leave them to indulge in vain idle talk and amuse themselves, until they face the Day which they have been promised,

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: So leave them to plunge in vain talk and play about, until they encounter that Day of theirs which they have been promised!-

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: Fatharhum yakhoodoo wayalAAaboo hatta yulaqoo yawmahumu allathee yooAAadoona

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: The day when they will come out of their graves in all haste as though rushing to their altars,

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: A day when they will come out of the graves in haste, as if rushing towards their goals.

43.author: Arberry
43.text: the day they shall come forth from the tombs hastily, as if they were hurrying unto a waymark,

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: The Day whereon they shall come forth from the sepulchres hastily, as though they were to an altar hurrying.

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: The Day when they will come out of the graves quickly as racing to a goal,

43.author: Itani
43.text: The Day when they will emerge from the tombs in a rush, as though they were hurrying towards a target.

43.author: Maududi
43.text: the Day on which they will hastily come forth from their graves, as though they were hurrying on to the altars of their deities.

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: The Day when they will come out of the graves quickly as racing to a Nusub,

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: The day when they come forth from the graves in haste, as racing to a goal,

43.author: Qarai
43.text: the day when they emerge from graves, hastening, as if racing toward a target,

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: the Day when they shall rush from their graves, as if they were hurrying to a raised flag,

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: The Day they will emerge from the graves rapidly as if they were, toward an erected idol, hastening.

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: the Day when they rush out of their graves as if racing towards a signpost,

43.author: Shakir
43.text: The day on which they shall come forth from their graves in haste, as if they were hastening on to a goal,

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: the Day when they shall come out of their graves in haste, as if they were racing to a goal,

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: The Day whereon they will issue from their sepulchres in sudden haste as if they were rushing to a goal-post (fixed for them),-

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Yawma yakhrujoona mina alajdathi siraAAan kaannahum ila nusubin yoofidoona

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: Eyes lowered, shame attending. That is the day they have been promised!

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: With lowered eyes, disgrace overcoming them; this is the day, which they had been promised.

44.author: Arberry
44.text: humbled their eyes, overspreading them abasement. That is the day which they were promised.

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Downcast shall be their looks; abjectness shall overspread them. Such is the Day which they are promised.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: With their eyes lowered in fear and humility, ignominy covering them (all over)! That is the Day which they were promised!

44.author: Itani
44.text: Their eyes cast down; overwhelmed by humiliation. This is the Day which they were promised.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: Their eyes will be downcast and disgrace will overwhelm them. Such is the Day that they were promised.

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: With their eyes lowered in fear and humility, ignominy covering them (all over)! That is the Day which they were promised!

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: With eyes aghast, abasement stupefying them: Such is the Day which they are promised.

44.author: Qarai
44.text: with a humbled look [in their eyes], overcast by abasement. That is the day they had been promised.

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: their eyes are humbled and they are covered with humiliation. Such is the Day that they were promised.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: Their eyes humbled, humiliation will cover them. That is the Day which they had been promised.

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: with their eyes cast down and covered by disgrace; the day about which they were promised.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: Their eyes cast down; disgrace shall overtake them; that is the day which they were threatened with.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: with downcast eyes and faces distorted in shame; such is the Day which they are promised.

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: Their eyes lowered in dejection,- ignominy covering them (all over)! such is the Day the which they are promised!

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: KhashiAAatan absaruhum tarhaquhum thillatun thalika alyawmu allathee kanoo yooAAadoona
